steps.title: Restate the claim
steps.content: Toomer establishes a contrast between the narrator's attitude toward life and Avey's. The quote must explicitly contrast their attitudes.

steps.title: Match the quotation
steps.content:
Choice B: "As time went on, [Avey's] indifference to things began to pique me; I was ambitious, I left earlier than she did." That contrasts Avey's indifference with the narrator's ambition — exactly the contrast in attitudes toward life.

  • A is about her smile
  • C describes only the narrator
  • D describes only Avey
. None creates a direct life-attitude contrast.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
